session: akiendelllinux_cc_0 — opened with /loadslate against 2026-07-22-charter-placement-and-summarized-design, after the system restart Akien named at that slate's close. Long, high-yield session: one doc deliverable, one ticket child closed, one host change made AND retroactively intentioned, and a structural restructure of the commons that fell out of the retroactive intentioning. Closed by /intent on three nodes and this slate, at Akien's call to capture and compact.
author: CC
in_flight:
NOTHING is mid-build. Everything below is committed and PUSHED except the three notes/slate written at close (committed with this slate). WHAT LANDED, in order:

(1) TAKE-TO-WORK DOC — ~/.akien/20260722.AkienOnWhereThingsStand.draft3.md (NOT a git repo; a path, not a commit). Draft 2 untouched. Added the status-tag reading key the 2026-07-20 slate claimed had landed but hadn't (MEASURED/IN BETA/READY/STARTED/ENVISIONED/HYPOTHESIS + what DD vs AM means). Inference proxy moved ENVISIONED -> MEASURED: both new proofs re-run green that morning (inference_domain: compile-once/verified-before-served/owner-gated/metered spent-vs-avoided; charter/projector). Three new paragraphs in 'Design by intent' (provenance beside the code; history append-only with state compiled from it; a charter is a SUMMARY not an intention). New closing section 'The bet underneath' — the CS-challenge framing with its falsifier, placed LAST to keep Akien's deliberate concrete->speculative order. Typos fixed. The pre-build whitepaper needed no update.

(2) CHILD (d) of charter-state-history-split RESOLVED — cairn 89a06d8 + a17c25d, CairnCommons 4e5170b. CLAUDE.md's 'code only, no state, ever' was FALSE after the placement ruling and is now 'code and the record of how it got here; no *runtime* state'. Law 5 restated: 'Intent, its voyage, and its proofs share an address.' MAP gained a 'What a charter is' section (two cadences + three-scale recursion + the middle row left open) and a 'Where they live' stone under VALIDATIONS. The db_domain IOU was WRONG (not just stale) and narrowed to relational/graph-tree state; one new IOU added — 'a compiled view is never hand-edited' — which technically breaks that section's 'shrinks monotonically' claim (flagged, unresolved). ALSO corrected against disk: MAP's repo tree showed cairn/devices/<name>/; devices sit directly under cairn/, no devices/ layer, and bus/charter/system_rackmount/sudo_relay were missing entirely.

(3) THE USAGE-WIDGET HOOK — built on the host, then intentioned retroactively when Akien caught the miss ('we prolly should have intentioned that first'). Script was already installed and is a PATCHED copy (env-var passing; the plasmoid ships a version that interpolates into a Python string literal and breaks on quotes) — do NOT re-copy from the plasmoid dir on upgrade. Missing piece was the hook registration: Stop + StopFailure in ~/.claude/settings.json, matcher "", timeout 15. Pipe-tested (cache written, plan default_claude_max_5x, 12 usage windows); schema-validated both events; Akien confirmed the live hook fires.

(4) host-seam + intentions-other/ FOUNDED — CairnCommons 7ce266d, cairn a17c25d. The retroactive intentioning had nowhere to berth, which surfaced the gap: Law 5 assumes every implementation HAS an address we own, and a hook in ~/.claude/settings.json does not. Coined `host-seam` (parallel to code-seam; the seam is between Cairn and the machine under it) with two gates code-seams don't need because git can't freeze a machine: a REBUILD gate (carries an `apply` recipe complete enough to bring a bare box to this state from the node alone) and a RE-VERIFY gate (the seal EXPIRES — the host drifts with nothing in git changing). It learns by reading that drift. First member: usage-widget-hook, carrying its recipe and a monthly verify; its honest falsifier is SILENCE — the script exits 0 on every failure path, so a dead hook looks exactly like a healthy one.

(5) THE ASSEMBLY-POINT RESTRUCTURE — CairnCommons 4cf8cf9 + f65037d, cairn df49191 + 3dc5545. Akien's framing: `intentions/` is COMPILED from `intentions-other/` PLUS the charters homed beside the code. So everything hand-authored left intentions/: first the ROOTS (telos.md, core-values.md), then the four spanning I-*.md. intentions/ now holds only a charter declaring it is a compiled view that is never hand-authored. THE ROOTS ARE FIRST BY TIMESTAMP (both 2026-07-14, the founding day), so a timestamp-ordered compile puts the frame at the top of the scan by PHYSICS, not by a special case — which is why there is no `values/` container (Akien floated one; it would have broken the seed claim, since carrying intentions/ to a bare machine would leave the frame behind). CROSS-REPO TRIPWIRE, caught and measured: test_core_values.py:40 hardcoded the old commons path and ABSTAINS on a miss — an abstain still prints green, so a stale path would have quietly RETIRED the drift gate rather than breaking it. Path fixed; re-measured 'commons drift gate bit'. Thirteen live pointers followed the moves (5 module docstrings, 3 charters' traces_to, MAP x2, CLAUDE.md x2, 1 ticket); slates left frozen (Law 7).

(6) THE PARTITION RULE, SHARPENED TWICE. Morning version: 'does this intention have code?' Wrong twice over. First correction — core-values LOOKS like it has code (cairn/base/core_values.py, pin-tested), but that module is the ENFORCED form, a canary; Law 2 holds CP1-CP6 EVERYWHERE, and a thing that holds everywhere has no single address. Second correction — I-heartbeat-callbacks-and-bus.md has code in FIVE files (ground_loop, bus, base/shim, base/callback, system_rackmount) and therefore nowhere to be beside. FINAL RULE: 'does it have ONE code address?' Homeless means no address OR many. The beside-code charter answers 'what is THIS component for'; a spanning intention answers 'what is this ARRANGEMENT for', and the second question has no directory.
next_direction:
RESUME POINT: three nodes were born via /intent and are UN-CAST — nothing filed, no children spawned, no gates bound. /sorted is the next move on them. Captured in full at notes/cairn-command-and-bin.json.

(A) THE REPO SCANNER — a device that scans the repo and fires CALLBACKS on matches, calling the intentions-folder updater. This is the system-scale projector arriving from a better direction: a scan-match IS a trigger (any predicate, evaluated where its data is owned) and the update IS a callback, so base/callback.py + base/shim.py (both built, green) already carry it. Likely mostly WIRING, which would make it a candidate first member of `operational-driver` — the class defined 2026-07-18 with zero members to date.

(B) THE INTENTIONS-FOLDER UPDATER — one writer, three callers (the scanner's callback, /sorted, the command line). This CLOSES the IOU written into intentions/_charter+why.json the same afternoon: 'a compiled view is never hand-edited' is prose today because nothing enforces it. The updater as SOLE WRITER is the enforcement — the db_domain shape, no other door.

(C) THE BIN DISPATCHER — command name is `cairn`, APPROVED (measured: cc is taken by /usr/bin/cc and shadowed by a dangling ~/.local/bin/cc -> TheIgors). Dispatch is `verb=$1; shift; exec "$dir/$verb" "$@"` (Akien: CC++ — UU's $1..$9 form caps at nine args and shreds quoting). Commands are named `*.cairnshellcommand` so `ls *.cairnshellcommand` IS the discovery query and help COMPILES from each command's intention file. bin/ REFUSES a script with no charter — a gate, not a folder. The updater links cairn/bin into ~/.local/bin (not ~/bin) and yanks stale links.

Still open from the prior slate, unblocked and unbuilt: charter-state-history-split child (c) — migrate existing charters to state+history, inference_domain LAST by its own frozen ruling; and the spawned sibling to migrate the `validations` table out of db_domain to beside-code git. web_server children a/b/c untouched since 2026-07-20. The parent ticket sits at PROVEME and is not PROVED until (c) lands.
open_threads: MAINTENANCE — AXIS OR CLASS? Akien's find, full text at notes/maintenance-is-an-axis-not-a-class.json, AWAITING HIS RULING. He: maintenance work has intentions/whys/proofs, becomes a workflow, and when the workflow completes the intention need not remain — either really done, or recurrent, in which case the completed workflow goes to a logging depot (30-day or permanent flag) and the next trigger spawns a fresh ticket. CC's read, offered not settled: it may be an AXIS not a fifth class, because it cuts ACROSS classes (yanking symlinks is a host-seam; pruning stale tickets is repo work) and a node_class must answer where-it-berths and what-gate-proves-it, which maintenance doesn't answer consistently. The axis: WHAT BECOMES OF THE TICKET ON RESOLVE — FREEZE (into history; it is provenance) vs DEPOT (a receipt with a retention flag). The retention flag may need no invention: 'if losing it loses knowledge, it's commons' already decides it — permanent -> CairnCommons, 30-day -> ~/.cairn/ where logs live by law. CC'S ONE PUSHBACK: 'the intention does not need to remain' contradicts the standing hold that intentions are never closed and nothing is terminal. Cleaner reading that keeps it: the INTENTION is standing, the TICKET is the disposable instance, and a one-shot is a standing intention whose trigger will never fire again (UU stopped being a live debris source; the intention survives with an empty member set). If the intention genuinely evaporates, that is an EXCEPTION to a stated principle and must be recorded loudly, not absorbed.

IS bin/ CLASS-SPACE OR IS IT `launchers/` GROWN UP? Needs Akien. `launchers/` already exists doing symlink-onto-PATH work, and two folders doing one job is exactly the drift this system exists to prevent. Related small open: how a `*.cairnshellcommand` pairs with its intention — filename convention (help.cairnshellcommand + help.intention+why.json) or a directory per verb. Law 5 at file granularity; directory form matches components, flat form keeps bin/ scannable with one ls.

MEASURED HOST DEBRIS, 2026-07-22, awaiting the yank (this is node (C)'s first real job, and the example that produced the maintenance question). DANGLING: ~/.local/bin/cc -> TheIgors/claudecode/cc.sh; ~/bin/igor-worker; ~/bin/rescue-igor; ~/bin/typingmind; ~/bin/uucompactclaude. LIVE but pointing at the quarry: ~/bin/igor -> UnseenUniversity/igor. Also present in ~/bin: uuhelp, uumytickets, _igor_common.sh/.ps1, igor.ps1, superclaude.ps1, compact-igor-type. LIVE AND CORRECT, do not touch: ~/.local/bin/superclaude -> cairn/launchers/superclaude. Third-party, leave alone: claude, uv, uvx. ALSO: ~/.local/bin appears TWICE in PATH.

THE SYSTEM-SCALE PROJECTOR DOES NOT EXIST — said plainly in intentions/_charter+why.json rather than implied by the folder's emptiness. The charter-scale projector is built and green (cairn/charter/projector.py); this scale is not. So 'never hand-edit intentions/' is prose, and the folder is empty BY CONVENTION — the discipline-instead-of-physics that Cairn exists to stop trusting. Nodes (A)+(B) are what close it.

THE SEED CLAIM IS A RUNNABLE EXPERIMENT, not a metaphor: bare box, the intentions folder, nothing else. It is simultaneously the strongest proof this system could produce and the cleanest falsifier for the whole bet. Worth casting as a real proof once intentions-other/ holds enough to make it non-trivial. Today it holds 7 records.

HONESTY DEBT, STILL UNPAID and now INCONSISTENT: node_classes/code-seam.json members_so_far still lists only default-superclaude-to-200k (missing db_domain, inference_domain, web-server, harbor-master, charter-state-history-split). I maintained host-seam's members_so_far, so the two classes now disagree about whether that field is hand-kept. Given where this session went, the answer is probably that it should be COMPILED like everything else — which folds it into node (A).

OPS NOTE: gh's token was invalid after the restart and hung a push on a kwallet prompt; Akien re-authed in another window (keyring, scopes gist/read:org/repo/workflow). Both repos even with origin at close. Unrelated: ~/.akien/akien.credentials.cfg holds a GitHub PAT in plaintext — intentional per Akien (akien creds vs igor creds), and in UU these lived in the vault device in the db. THERE IS NO VAULT DEVICE IN CAIRN YET and it is not on the spine; a credential store is exactly the shape Cairn is good at (one owner, one gate, no ambient reach). Possibly an instance of MAP's open Q3.
